content:
- Updated the Ralph task file with completed execution notes and checkboxes, set passes=true, and ran .ralph/task_switch.sh successfully.
- Next I am reviewing the final git status, then I will add all modified files including .ralph state, commit with the required task-finished format, push, and stop.

- Started Choose-Task run.
- Read progress log; nothing useful was emitted, so I am reconstructing state from Ralph task files.
- Next I am reading current_tasks and current_tasks_done to resolve blockers and strict precedence before writing current_task.txt.

- Parsed current_tasks.md and current_tasks_done.md.
- Checked every active candidate task file for blocker and priority tags.
- Selection is forced by bug-first precedence: `.ralph/tasks/bugs/no-quorum-integrity-check-does-not-verify-committed-keys.md` is the only active unblocked failing bug, so it must be chosen next.

- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Selected `.ralph/tasks/bugs/no-quorum-integrity-check-does-not-verify-committed-keys.md` because it is the only active unblocked failing bug and bug-first precedence is mandatory.
- Quitting immediately after the required write.

- Read previous Ralph progress and current task pointer.
- The active task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/no-quorum-integrity-check-does-not-verify-committed-keys.md.
- Next I am reading the task file to determine whether it is at plan, verify, or execute stage.

- The task file is still unplanned, so this turn must produce the detailed plan and stop at TO BE VERIFIED.
- I am searching for the no-quorum test, the integrity helper, and where committed keys are recorded.
- Next I will inspect the relevant test/support files and then patch the task markdown with the full plan.

- The no-quorum test code already appears to include the stronger recovery key-set verification path.
- I am checking git status and diffs so the written plan reflects the actual worktree, not just HEAD or the stale task description.
- Next I will decide whether the plan should cover implementation work, verification-only work, or task-state reconciliation.

- HEAD already has commit 369369a titled "task finished failsafe-integrity-ultra-long-flake: strengthen no-quorum recovery verification".
- The bug logic in tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs already matches the task description, so the remaining gap is Ralph task-state reconciliation.
- I am checking existing task markdown patterns before patching this task file with a detailed plan and the required TO BE VERIFIED marker.

- Wrote a detailed execution plan into .ralph/tasks/bugs/no-quorum-integrity-check-does-not-verify-committed-keys.md and ended it with TO BE VERIFIED.
- The plan captures the critical discovery that current HEAD already appears to implement the stronger no-quorum recovery key-set verification, so the next pass must skeptically validate that baseline and reconcile stale task state.
- I am stopping immediately now, per instructions, leaving the task in TO BE VERIFIED state for the next engineer/turn.

- Reviewed .ralph/tasks/bugs/no-quorum-integrity-check-does-not-verify-committed-keys.md in TO BE VERIFIED state and did a skeptical code read of tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs.
- Confirmed the recovery key integrity fix is already present in both HEAD and origin/master via commit 369369a, including the recovered-primary verification path, committed-key bound helper, and focused unit tests.
- Revised the task plan materially: execution must now treat this as stale task-state reconciliation unless targeted verification disproves the landed implementation; changed the task file terminator from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next turn should not re-explore. It should execute the written plan: run the required gates, update task state based on results, make any necessary reconciliation/doc changes, then finish the Ralph completion flow only if all commands pass.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it is in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Starting execution exactly from the written plan: targeted baseline verification, focused tests, then make check/test/test-long/lint.
- Worktree already has unrelated Ralph state changes; I will leave them intact and only add the required task completion updates if all gates pass.

- Verified the no-quorum scenario wiring in tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs: it computes required/allowed committed key sets and verifies the recovered stable primary with assert_table_recovery_key_integrity_on_node.
- My first focused cargo test command used the wrong test target name; corrected course toward the actual ha_multi_node_stress target instead of broadening scope.
- Next I will run the specific helper/cutoff unit tests from that target, then the required make gates.

- Confirmed the focused helper coverage still passes on the actual ha_multi_node_stress target: 4 recovery-bound tests and 1 cutoff-alignment test all green.
- No code change has been necessary so far; the task remains stale-state reconciliation plus required gate verification unless the full make gates fail.
- Beginning the required full gate sequence now in the task-mandated order: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.

- Focused committed-key helper tests passed, then make check passed and make test completed with 422 passed / 9 profile-skipped.
- make test-long is in progress now; early results show the first ultra-long failover and failsafe scenarios already passed.
- If test-long and lint finish green, the remaining work is task markdown reconciliation, Ralph task switch, commit, and push.

- make test-long disproved the stale-state assumption: the exact no-quorum fencing integrity scenario failed in ultra-long with missing required recovered keys.
- Failure message shows recovered key-set mismatch on node-2 with four missing required keys and no unexpected keys, which suggests the current cutoff/required-key invariant is too strict or recovery verification timing is wrong under real execution.
- I am letting the rest of the ultra-long profile finish for complete gate context, then I will inspect the scenario artifact summary/timeline and patch the implementation in one pass.

- Patched fail-safe recovery so a node that is still the PostgreSQL primary when quorum returns re-enters the normal primary decision path instead of re-fencing itself.
- Updated HA decide/worker unit tests to expect leadership reacquisition rather than a release-and-fence loop after quorum restoration.
- Running the exact previously failing ultra-long no-quorum fencing integrity scenario now before rerunning the full gate sequence.

- The direct no-quorum ultra-long scenario now passes after the fail-safe recovery change.
- Restarted the required gate sequence from scratch; make check is green again and make test is currently running.
- If the full gate set stays green, I still need to reconcile the task markdown, switch Ralph tasks, commit, and push.

- After updating the worker contract test, make test now passes cleanly again: 422 passed, 9 profile-skipped.
- Running make test-long for the full ultra-long HA suite once more to validate the no-quorum fix under the required gate.
- Remaining required work after test-long is make lint, then task-state completion, Ralph switch, commit, and push.

- All required gates are green after the fix: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Updated the task markdown to done/true with acceptance boxes checked and execution notes describing the real fail-safe recovery bug and fix.
- Running .ralph/task_switch.sh now, then I will commit all tracked Ralph/code changes and push.
